title: UFO reverse-engineering in Philip J. Corso's The Day After Roswell
summary: This interview excerpt discusses Philip J. Corso's book 'The Day After Roswell' and his claims about reverse-engineering technology from the Roswell crash. Corso allegedly claims a covert government group was formed to collect off-planet technology debris, leading to developments like fiber optics, lasers, integrated circuits, and Kevlar.

acronyms.definition: United States intelligence agency; mentioned in context of Admiral Hillenkoetter being its first director

key_claims.claim: Philip J. Corso was on the staff of President Eisenhower's National Security Council for 4 years
A covert government group was assembled under Admiral Roscoe H. Hillenkoetter to collect all information on off-planet technology and retrieve debris from the Roswell crash
Reverse engineering of Roswell parts indirectly led to the development of fiber optics, lasers, integrated circuits, and Kevlar
